content:
had to triple-check this when i saw it hit $100/month

first time seeing that number for my own apps. feels different than reading case studies online.

what changed: stopped building features nobody asked for, and started charging the people who'd already been using it free

saw this on reddit: "how are you getting users with $0 marketing budget"

the real answer nobody wants to hear: you can't outrun the product.

either:
1. your product solves a problem people are actively looking for (search)
2. people naturally tell their friends (viral)
3. you built an audience first (distribution)

pick one, stop picking zero

someone taught claude to "talk like a caveman" to cut token usage 75%

me: "HOW"

them: "fewer fancy words. fewer commas. cave no need complex. cave write direct"

it's ridiculous but it actually works. your prompt engineering style has a direct cost.
